title: cistus lasianthus subsp. lasianthus
intro: Cistus lasianthus subsp. lasianthus, also known as the Lasianthus Cistus or Eastwood's Rockrose, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, skin issues, and respiratory problems. The leaves and flowers of Cistus lasianthus subsp. lasianthus contain flavonoids, saponins, and terpenes, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits, such as antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. Cistus lasianthus subsp. lasianthus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, making it a popular ingredient in traditional herbal medicine.
